title: S&P 500 Companies Complete List (2026 Updated)
description: Complete, searchable list of all 503 S&P 500 companies in 2026. Includes market cap, P/E ratio, dividend yield, sector, and YTD return data.

mainEntity.name: How many companies are in the S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: Although it is called the S&P 500, the index currently contains 503 constituents because several companies have multiple share classes listed (like Alphabet's GOOG and GOOGL).

mainEntity.name: What is the largest company in the S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: The largest companies by market capitalization in the S&P 500 currently fluctuate between Microsoft, Apple, and NVIDIA, often exceeding $3 trillion in market value.

mainEntity.name: How are companies selected for the S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: To be eligible for the S&P 500, a company must be a U.S. company, have a market capitalization of at least $18 billion, be highly liquid, have a public float of at least 10% of its shares outstanding, and have positive earnings in the most recent quarter and over the four most recent quarters in sum.

mainEntity.name: What sectors make up the S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S&P 500 is divided into 11 GICS sectors: Information Technology, Health Care, Financials, Consumer Discretionary, Communication Services, Industrials, Consumer Staples, Energy, Utilities, Real Estate, and Materials.

mainEntity.name: How often does the S&P 500 change?

acceptedAnswer.text: The index is rebalanced quarterly, but constituents can be added or removed throughout the year due to mergers, acquisitions, or companies no longer meeting eligibility criteria.
